mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing price-to-earnings multiples and generating negative forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >55% of months following rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.3pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer spending power, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing slowdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a hit rate below 40% over a 24-month evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, the cost of capital becomes prohibitive for highly leveraged firms, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of months when the federal funds rate stays above 4.0 percent, invalidating the cost-of-capital transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction effect shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which boosts near-term corporate revenue expectations and generates positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window, or positive housing start readings are followed by negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index reading above 105.0 during a disinflationary period signals resilient manufacturing capacity that outpaces demand destruction, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of triggered months, or the regime filter fails to improve out-of-sample Sharpe ratio compared to unconditional trading.
